summary: The DHC-6 primary flight controls are conventional mechanical systems — cables, pulleys, and linkages — from either seat via dual-Y control column and rudder pedals. Trim tabs on left elevator, left aileron, and rudder provide force relief. Geared servo tabs on each aileron and the rudder give aerodynamic assistance. The right elevator carries an automatic flap-elevator interconnect trim tab. Secondary controls are hydraulically actuated flaps. Stall warning: two leading-edge vanes (left wing) plus a mechanical stall bar.

modificationVariants.name: Vortex Generators
modificationVariants.description: On both sides of the vertical stabilizer and under the horizontal stabilizer. Enhance rudder/elevator effectiveness at low airspeed. Must not be bent during washing.

modificationVariants.name: Stall Warning Horn
modificationVariants.description: Auditory horn simultaneous with STALL light. Earlier aircraft may be light-only.

components.name: Ailerons
components.description: Conventional differential ailerons. Movement differential increases with flap deflection. Spring-loaded to neutral. Left aileron: geared servo tab + electric trim tab. Right aileron: geared servo tab only.

components.name: Elevators
components.description: Two mechanical elevators with wiper seals for low-speed performance. Left elevator: manually adjustable trim tab. Right elevator: automatic flap-elevator interconnect trim tab (no crew control).

components.name: Flap-Elevator Interconnect Trim Tab
components.description: Fully automatic mechanical linkage between flap actuator and right elevator trim tab. Compensates for pitch change during flap operation. Cannot be overridden from cockpit.

components.name: Rudder
components.description: Mechanical from interconnected pedals. Wiper seal. Upper trailing edge: manually adjustable trim tab. Lower trailing edge: geared servo tab (opposite to rudder deflection). Pedals also function as independent brake pedals.

components.name: Wing Flaps
components.description: Hydraulically actuated split flaps. Positions 10°/20°/30°/40° — no detents, visual check required. Flap position indicator on windshield centre post. Ailerons droop with flap extension. Flaps extend faster than retract by design.

components.name: Control Column and Wheels
components.description: Dual-Y at aircraft centreline. Control wheels interconnected via chain-and-sprocket. Fore/aft for elevators. Nose wheel steering lever on left wheel hub. Mic/intercom switches on each wheel.

components.name: Stall Warning Vanes
components.description: Two leading-edge vanes on left wing. Lower vane always active; upper vane active only with flaps >12°. Illuminates red STALL light and horn 4–9 kts above stall.

components.name: Stall Bar
components.description: Metal bar on left wing leading edge. Disrupts airflow at high AoA causing a column buffet warning. Float aircraft: additional bar on right wing. MANDATORY for dispatch — cannot be deferred.

controls.name: Elevator Trim Wheel
controls.description: Mechanical jackscrew. White band = take-off range.

controls.name: Aileron Trim Switch
controls.description: Electric, spring-loaded to OFF. Runaway: operate switch in appropriate direction, pull AIL TRIM ACT CB when force relieved.

controls.name: Rudder Trim Wheel
controls.description: Mechanical. T/O: slightly right of centre.

controls.name: Flap Selector Lever
controls.description: Overhead console, no detents. Locking device must be released before movement.
